mechanism_narrative: A sustained upward trend in FEDFUNDS over the prior 6 months raises the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, compressing valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days, independent of any threshold crossing.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC between FEDFUNDS trend slope and forward 20d returns stays below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or returns following rate increases are not significantly more negative than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE falls and holds below 4.0% for three consecutive months, the labor market is tight enough to trigger wage-inflation fears that raise expected future Fed tightening, causing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days to be negative relative to periods with higher unemployment.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: Mean 60d forward return during triggered low-unemployment periods is not significantly below the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 across 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Strong RSAFS growth paired with a simultaneous decline in HOUST indicates a bifurcated demand environment where rate-sensitive housing activity weakens while non-durable retail spending stays elevated; this divergence signals the leading edge of monetary tightening transmission and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as housing weakness eventually spreads to broader demand.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1282k asof 2018-08 declining from cycle highs
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with diverging demand components
failure_condition: When both trigger conditions are met, forward 60d returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the interaction term's marginal predictive contribution over either single series alone is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A persistent upward trend in INDPRO reflects expanding industrial capacity utilization and corporate output, which mechanically supports forward earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as an always-on momentum signal.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO 3-month momentum and forward 20d returns falls below 0.03 across any 2-year rolling window, or positive INDPRO momentum periods do not outperform negative momentum periods (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime, a joint pattern of accelerating CPIAUCSL inflation and rising FEDFUNDS signals that the Fed is behind the inflation curve and must tighten more aggressively than markets expect, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days; this mechanism is muted or reversed in early-cycle or disinflationary regimes.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY growth > 2.5% AND FEDFUNDS rising 3-month trend
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 251.994 asof 2018-09 with FEDFUNDS at 1.95% and rising
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with monetary tightening and rising inflation
failure_condition: During periods satisfying both trigger conditions in a late-cycle regime, forward 60d returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05), or the effect reverses sign when tested in an independent late-cycle tightening episode.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month momentum in PAYEMS reflects continued labor income generation that supports near-term consumer spending and risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as an always-on short-horizon signal.
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS monthly change and forward 5d returns is below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive PAYEMS surprises do not produce higher subsequent 5d returns than negative surprises (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in HOUST exceeding 10% from its trailing 12-month peak signals a meaningful contraction in rate-sensitive housing demand, acting as a leading indicator of broader consumption slowdown that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days once the threshold is crossed.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines more than 10% from trailing 12-month peak
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1282k asof 2018-08 down from earlier 2018 peaks
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle affecting housing-sensitive demand
failure_condition: Forward 60d returns following a triggered HOUST decline are positive in more than 55% of historical instances, or Rank IC conditional on the trigger is below 0.03 across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: In an environment of tight labor markets (low UNRATE) combined with decelerating INDPRO growth, the interaction signals capacity constraints binding on output even as demand for labor remains strong, foreshadowing margin compression and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days; this mechanism is only active late in the business cycle.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE < 4.0% AND INDPRO 3-month growth decelerates below prior 6-month average
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.7% and INDPRO growth showing signs of moderation asof 2018-08/09
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with binding capacity constraints
failure_condition: When both conditions are jointly triggered, forward 20d returns are not significantly negative relative to unconditional baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the interaction effect disappears when tested outside late-cycle regimes.
